title: selaginella squarrosa
intro: Selaginella squarrosa, also known as the "water fern," is a species of evergreen, perennial plant in the family Selaginellaceae, characterized by its small, scale-like leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Selaginella squarrosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and topical creams, although it is essential to consult with a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
